context: Uncommitted changes modify components/game/leaderboard-card.tsx and app/game/page.tsx (a new leaderboard sidebar) plus its CSS. The project has a dev server script and Playwright installed. bruhs:state has Linear configured. This is a user-visible UI change.
expected_behavior: Step 2 categorizes as 'feat' and generates a Conventional Commits title ('feat(game): add leaderboard sidebar'), and Step 6 commits with a Conventional Commits message

Step 7.5 fires because the diff matches UI surface (.tsx/.css): it runs `/expect` to verify the leaderboard in a real browser via the chrome-devtools MCP (renders, sorts by win rate, empty state, mobile viewport), and fixes + re-verifies if /expect finds breakage before capturing anything

After verification, it captures screenshots at desktop 1280x800 and mobile 390x844 AND records a short screen recording of the changed interaction (e.g. the leaderboard updating), via chrome-devtools MCP or the project's Playwright (recordVideo + page.screenshot) as a fallback, saved under /tmp/bruhs-ui-preview, following practices/ui-preview.md

Step 8 opens the PR with a `## UI preview` section containing: a 'Verified with /expect (chrome-devtools)' line, the screenshots embedded via hosted URLs (e.g. raw.githubusercontent on the bruhs-assets branch), and the recording (linked, with a note that an inline player needs a manual drag-drop)
The PR body honestly states the hosting path used and any remaining manual step for inline video playback rather than claiming a CLI upload produced an inline player
Cleanup: stops the dev server if yeet started it and removes /tmp/bruhs-ui-preview after embedding
failure_modes: Skips Step 7.5 entirely and opens the UI PR with no preview or a bare `<screenshot>` placeholder
Embeds screenshots without running /expect first — a preview of an unverified UI
Captures only a static screenshot and no screen recording of the interaction
Claims the recording renders inline as a video player from a gh/CLI upload (it does not — only web-composer user-attachments inline-play); hallucinating a working hosting path
References a local /tmp path in the PR body (renders as nothing for reviewers) instead of a hosted URL
Writes a non-Conventional commit message or PR title
